fix: sanitize absolute runner paths in generated schemas
prs.body:
**Notes for Reviewers**
This PR fixes #526
This PR addresses the issue of absolute environment paths being leaked into the repository during the automated type generation process. Currently, the GitHub Action runner’s local workspace path (e.g., /home/runner/work/...) is being captured by the generation tool and committed directly into the TypeScript schemas, such as SchemasOpenApiSchema.ts.

This causes unnecessary git noise and pollutes the codebase with environment-specific metadata that is irrelevant to other developers and production environments.

**Changes**

•Workflow Sanitization: Updated the generate-types workflow to include a post-generation cleanup step. This step dynamically identifies the $GITHUB_WORKSPACE and replaces it with a relative ./ path across all generated .ts files.

•Verification Gate: Added a validation check before the commit step. The CI will now scan for any remaining /home/runner strings and fail the build if a path leak is detected.

•Standardized Error Logging: Integrated a descriptive error moniker for the verification failure to align with the project's event-tracking framework.
- [✅] Yes, I signed my commits.

prs.title: fixed (mesheryctl): correct system config minikube behavior with Docker
prs.body:
**Notes for Reviewers**
- This PR fixes #16664
This PR fixes the issue where the mesheryctl system config minikube command fails when using the Docker Meshery Extension. It ensures that the CLI correctly detects and configures Minikube contexts in Docker environments and properly uploads kubeconfig data to the Meshery server.

Problem
Users running Minikube with Docker Desktop and the Meshery Docker Extension have experienced failures when executing:
`mesheryctl system config minikube`
This is due to incorrect handling of kubeconfig flattening and transport when invoked in Docker contexts. The command should properly flatten the Minikube kubeconfig and send it to the Meshery server regardless of deployment type (Kubernetes or Docker). 

**Changes in This PR**

Added support for handling Minikube contexts when running from the Docker Meshery Extension environment.
Ensured kubeconfig is minified and flattened before upload.
Normalized handling of multipart/form-data for the kubeconfig upload.
Added unit tests validating system config minikube in both native and Docker extension contexts.
Updated CLI documentation to reflect the supported flow for Docker Extension users.

**[Signed commits](https://github.com/meshery/meshery/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#signing-off-on-commits-developer-certificate-of-origin)**
- [✅ ] Yes, I signed my commits.

prs.title: Improved CRD discovery to automatically scan repositories
prs.body:
**Description**
This PR fixes #881 

**Notes for Reviewers**
This PR enhances the CRD discovery mechanism by allowing users to specify a repository URL as the source instead of a static directory path. The generator will automatically discover CRDs within the repository, regardless of their location in the file structure.

**Implementation Details**
Introduces a more robust CRD discovery strategy inspired by the logic used in crdsdev/doc.
Replaces static directory assumptions with recursive repository scanning and pattern-based CRD detection.
Improves resilience against repository structure changes without impacting existing functionality.

**Benefits**
Reduces configuration complexity for users.
Makes CRD discovery resilient to repository refactors.
Improves usability and long-term maintainability of the model generator.

**Example Usage**
`Source: https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane`
The generator automatically locates CRDs without requiring users to specify internal paths.

**[Signed commits](../CONTRIBUTING.md#signing-off-on-commits-developer-certificate-of-origin)**
- [ ✅] Yes, I signed my commits.

prs.title: Model Generation : Added Support mapping of group field in CRDs
prs.body:
**Description**

This PR fixes #731 

**Current Behaviour**

Previously, the `group` field was treated as a regular property instead of a key identifier for associating custom resources with their API groups.

**Enhancement**

•Updated CRD parsing logic to recognize and use the group field.
•Implemented component-to-model mapping based on the API group value.
•Ensured compatibility with both native and custom Kubernetes resources.
•Improved maintainability by centralizing group-based mapping logic.

**Testing**

•Validated functionality with multiple CRDs, ensuring:
•Accurate mapping across different API groups.
•No regression in existing CRD handling.
•Successful parsing and model association for both standard and custom resources.

prs.title: Added script for data export/import functionality
prs.body:
Solves #476 
**This PR introduces a new Python-based script to handle data export and import operations for user data and project settings.**
*The script provides a simple command-line interface (CLI) that allows users to:
Export existing project data into a structured JSON file `(exported_data.json)` for backup or sharing.
Import data from a JSON file back into the system `(project_data.json)` for easy data restoration or transfer.*

**Key Features**
•Lightweight and user-friendly CLI interface.
•Safe read/write operations with validation checks.
•Fully self-contained — no external dependencies beyond Python’s standard library.
•Can be easily extended to support other formats (CSV, SQLite, etc.)

**How to Use**
1.Run the script:
`python data_transfer.py`

2.Choose one of the options:
`1 → Export data`
`2 → Import data`
`3 → Exit`

**Give Correct Naming for the files to be imported/exported.**

*Verified export/import with sample project_data.json, validated error handling for missing files, and confirmed JSON structure integrity after multiple cycles.*

prs.title: Solved Dp with Graph – Longest Increasing Path in a Matrix
prs.body:
This PR solves #8568 
*This adds a C++ implementation for the “Longest Increasing Path in a Matrix” problem.
The solution finds the length of the longest path in which each move goes to a cell with a strictly greater value, allowing moves in all four directions (up, down, left, right).*

**Example**
`[
  [9,9,4],
  [6,6,8],
  [2,1,1]
]`
**Output:**
`4`

prs.title: Added Sorting Techniques in Java
prs.body:
Solves #825
**This PR adds implementations of five fundamental sorting algorithms in Java.**
*Each algorithm is placed in its own file under the /Sorting/ directory.*
Following clean code principles with detailed inline comments and complexity analysis.

**Algorithms Implemented**

•Bubble Sort – Basic comparison-based sorting

•Insertion Sort – Efficient for small or nearly sorted arrays

•Selection Sort – Simple selection-based sorting algorithm

•Merge Sort – Divide and conquer sorting algorithm

•Quick Sort – Efficient divide and conquer sorting algorithm

prs.title: DRAFT: Enhancing Music Blocks: Sidebar Access, Real-Time Feedback & Interactive Learning
prs.body:
This PR is related to issue #4247 .

 **Summary of Changes:**
Added a Sidebar button in Music Blocks, allowing users to access a portal for learning about Music Blocks or teaching music.

**Planned Enhancements:**
1. Integrated Learning Access:
•Provide users with direct access to Music Blocks learning materials, either through an embedded portal or an in-page guide.

2. Real-time Feedback System:
•Implement a feedback mechanism that evaluates how users add blocks.
•Offer constructive feedback on their music creation process, helping them improve.

3. Interactive Chatbot:
•Develop a chatbot to assist users with learning and troubleshooting.
•Enable interactive guidance for users to better understand Music Blocks concepts.

These improvements aim to enhance the learning experience by making Music Blocks more interactive and user-friendly.

prs.title: Fix: Handle pygame.mixer initialization failure gracefully in systems without a sound device.
prs.body:
This PR fixes issue #20 .

Changes Made :

•Updated sugargame/canvas.py:
•Added a fallback to set SDL_AUDIODRIVER="dummy" if pygame.mixer fails.
•Re-raised exceptions for other modules to ensure proper error handling.

 Impact:

•Fixes crashes caused by pygame.mixer on no-sound systems.
•Ensures activities using Sugargame can run without sound functionality.

prs.title: Added support for echoing multiple fields in ros2 topic echo
prs.body:
#951 
# Now ros2 topic echo has support  for multiple fields
This PR extends the functionality of the ros2 topic echo command to allow users to specify and display multiple fields from a ROS topic message. Previously, the command supported either displaying the entire message or a single field using the --field argument. With this enhancement, users can specify multiple --field arguments to display specific fields, including nested fields.

prs.title: API: Created Systemd file so the app can run as a Linux system daemon.
prs.body:
#2737 
This File will help the APP to run on Linux System daemon.
If you want to check for the system .
# You can Run the below code as per order to check.
# Step-1:
 sudo cp examples/linux/systemd/talawa-api.service /etc/systemd/system/
# Step-2: 
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
# Step-3: 
sudo systemctl enable talawa-api.service
# Step-4: 
sudo systemctl start talawa-api.service
# Step-5: 
sudo systemctl status talawa-api.service
